questions.question: What is the function of a node in a plant?
questions.correct_answer: It is a swollen area on a stem where leaves, branches, or roots are attached.
questions.explanation: A node is a joint or a swelling on a stem that supports the attachment of leaves, branches, or roots.

questions.question: What is the purpose of an internode in a plant?
questions.correct_answer: It is the part of a stem between two nodes.
questions.explanation: An internode is the section of a stem between two nodes, providing support for the plant's growth.

questions.question: What is the function of an apical bud in a plant?
questions.correct_answer: It is responsible for the production of new leaves and flowers.
questions.explanation: An apical bud is located at the tip of a stem and produces new leaves and flowers.

questions.question: What is the difference between a simple leaf and a compound leaf?
questions.correct_answer: A simple leaf has a single blade, while a compound leaf has multiple blades.
questions.explanation: Simple leaves have a single blade, whereas compound leaves are made up of multiple blades.

questions.question: What is the structure of a flower?
questions.correct_answer: It consists of sepals, petals, stamens, and pistils.
questions.explanation: A flower has four main parts: sepals (protection), petals (attract pollinators), stamens (male reproductive organs), and pistils (female reproductive organs).

questions.question: What is the purpose of a petal in a flower?
questions.explanation: Petals are colorful structures that attract pollinators, such as bees and butterflies.

questions.question: What is the function of a stamen in a flower?
questions.explanation: A stamen is responsible for producing pollen, which is essential for fertilization and seed production.

questions.question: What is the structure of a fruit?
questions.correct_answer: It consists of seeds, pericarp, and mesocarp.
questions.explanation: A fruit has three main parts: seeds (encapsulate new plant growth), pericarp (outer layer), and mesocarp (middle layer).

questions.question: What is the purpose of a seed?
questions.explanation: A seed contains embryonic plant tissue, nutrients, and water, allowing it to develop into a new plant.

questions.question: What is the function of a root in a plant?
questions.correct_answer: It absorbs water and nutrients from the soil.
questions.explanation: A root anchors the plant, absorbs water, and takes up minerals from the surrounding soil.

notes.title: Types of Leaves
notes.content: There are three main types of leaves: simple, compound, and modified.

notes.title: Flower Structure
notes.content: A flower consists of sepals, petals, stamens, and pistils.

notes.title: Fruit Structure
notes.content: A fruit has three main parts: seeds, pericarp, and mesocarp.

notes.title: Seed Development
notes.content: Seeds develop from ovules within the ovary of a flower.

notes.title: Root Function
notes.content: A root absorbs water, nutrients, and minerals from the soil.

facts.fact: Plants can photosynthesize using light energy to produce glucose.
The largest flower in the world is the Rafflesia arnoldii.
Apples are a type of fruit that belongs to the rose family.
Seeds can remain dormant for years before germinating.
The tallest tree in the world is a coast redwood (Sequoia sempervirens).
